_comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the Interactive Globe -> Guided City Zoom lens. Edited by hand; consumed by BOTH tools/generate_map_textures.py (to capture the LOD PNGs) and Assets/Scripts/Globe/cityBounds.ts (to drive in-lens UV/scale/orientation). The generator regenerates cityBounds.ts from this file so the two can never drift. spanDeg is the LONGITUDE span (width) of the square crop in degrees; latitude is treated linearly (mercator distortion is negligible at city scale). L0 span is intentionally LARGE so the globe->table footprint handoff is forgiving.

globe._note: The globe uses a hand-imported equirectangular Earth texture (NOT generated by the tool). Bounds are full-planet for reference only.

transition._note: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the wide 'L-1' capture used ONLY to span-match the globe during the globe<->table handoff (it is NOT a navigable level; docking still lands at L0). For each city the tool captures a square covering spanMultiple x that city's L0 spanDeg, centered on L0, at outSize, and writes <city>_L-1.png. Change spanMultiple here (one place) to rescale every city's L-1.
